name: GameAssist
description:
# GameAssist

GameAssist is a modular Roll20 API automation suite for the **D&D 5E (2014 Character Sheet)**. It combines six independently managed modules with shared configuration, health reporting, and troubleshooting tools. DMs can use the full suite or enable only the features that fit their campaign.

## Features and Included Modules

**CritFumble**

Watches supported Roll20 attack templates for natural 1s, presents clear player choices, and rolls from campaign-specific melee, ranged, thrown, spell, or natural-attack fumble tables. GMs can also open a player picker manually or roll the martial and magic confirmation tables directly.

**ConcentrationTracker**

Builds normal, advantage, or disadvantage Constitution saves from incoming damage, remembers the most recent check, manages the configured concentration marker through TokenMod, and can show which current-page tokens are concentrating. Exact built-in and custom marker recognition helps keep token displays aligned with the game state.

**NPCManager**

Marks defeated NPCs, records revivals, audits HP and death-marker mismatches, and turns encounter history into readable Roll20 handouts organized by **Campaign > Chapter > Section > Session**. Custom Arc rosters can collect selected NPCs for character stories, vows, rivalries, investigations, redemption, or other campaign threads.

**NPCHPRoller**

Rolls HP from `npc_hpformula` for selected NPCs or qualifying NPCs on the current page. Optional automatic rolling handles newly added tokens while setup protection prevents placeholder HP from creating false death and revival history.

**ConfigUI**

Provides a GM-only chat panel for module controls, common settings, and readable summaries. Command-based configuration remains available for DMs who prefer it.

**DebugTools**

Provides optional, GM-only damage, marker, and saving-throw diagnostics. DebugTools starts disabled and uses dry-run previews unless the GM explicitly applies a change.

## Example at the Table

When a linked NPC falls from positive HP to 0, NPCManager records the death in the active Campaign, Chapter, Section, and Session buckets and asks TokenMod to add the configured death marker. If the NPC returns above 0 HP, GameAssist records the revival and removes the marker. The GM can review a short chat summary, audit the current page, or write the records to campaign handouts.

## Setup

1. Install GameAssist and its TokenMod dependency.
2. Create the seven CritFumble rollable tables if you plan to use CritFumble.
3. Save or restart the Roll20 Mod sandbox.
4. Run `!ga-status` for a readable system check.
5. Run `!ga-config ui` to open the GM settings panel.

## Main API Commands

- **System:** `!ga-status`, `!ga-status --details`, `!ga-config ui`, `!ga-config modules`, `!ga-enable`, `!ga-disable`
- **Critical misses:** `!critfumble help`, `!critfumble menu`, `!critfail`, `!critfumble-<type>`, `!confirm-crit-martial`, `!confirm-crit-magic`
- **Concentration:** `!concentration`, `!cc`, `!concentration --status`, `!ga-conc-status`
- **NPC history:** `!npc-death-report --help`, `!npc-death-buckets`, `!npc-death-write`, `!NPC-WR`, `!npc-death-audit`, `!npc-death-arc`
- **NPC hit points:** `!npc-hp-selected`, `!npc-hp-all`
- **Diagnostics:** `!ga-metrics`, `!ga-debug` after enabling DebugTools

GameAssist help panels explain the next choices in plain language. The full README contains the complete command matrix, examples, module settings, report workflows, upgrade guidance, and troubleshooting.

## Requirements and Compatibility

- **TokenMod 0.8.88** is the supported standalone baseline for NPCManager and ConcentrationTracker marker changes. GameAssist verifies requested marker results and reports failures without preventing death-history or concentration-status reads.
- **StatusInfo 0.3.11** is optional and can provide condition descriptions and menus alongside TokenMod.
- **CritFumble rollable tables:** `CF-Melee`, `CF-Ranged`, `CF-Thrown`, `CF-Spell`, `CF-Natural`, `Confirm-Crit-Martial`, and `Confirm-Crit-Magic`. GameAssist does not create or populate campaign-specific table results automatically.
- GameAssist v0.1.4.7 targets the **D&D 5E (2014) Character Sheet**. Features unrelated to an optional module or missing rollable table remain independently available.

## Setup and Documentation

See the [GameAssist README](https://github.com/Mord-Eagle/GameAssist#readme) for complete setup and operation. Bug reports and feature requests are welcome in [GameAssist Issues](https://github.com/Mord-Eagle/GameAssist/issues).
